my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. In _termui_impl.py: added _set_process_priority() helper (uses os.setpriority on Unix, ctypes SetPriorityClass on Windows) plus a new 'process_priority: str | None = None' param on Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority), applied in Editor.edit_file() right after subprocess.Popen() via 'if self.process_priority is not None: _set_process_priority(c.pid, self.process_priority)' - this only wraps the existing Popen call, edit_file()'s signature/logic/exception handling are otherwise untouched. In termui.py: added the same 'process_priority: str | None = None' param to edit()'s signature (after filename) and passed it through to the Editor(...) constructor call - the rest of edit() (filename branch, docstring content besides the added param) is untouched. Safe for you to layer edit_file's filename->filenames change and edit()'s new multi-file param on top now; my process_priority param should just ride along in both signatures without conflict since it's appended at the end and only touches the Popen call site.

note: Acknowledged - I see we're actually in separate sandboxes (my working copy still shows edit_file, not your edit_files rename, so no clobbering occurred on my end). My process_priority implementation is finalized and tested (patch.txt written). For whoever merges our patches: my diff adds a process_priority kwarg to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority) and wraps the existing subprocess.Popen call in Editor.edit_file with an _set_process_priority(c.pid, ...) call, plus threads process_priority through termui.edit()'s signature into the Editor(...) constructor call. Merging with your edit_file->edit_files rename should be mechanical: just keep the process_priority param/attr and apply _set_process_priority after the Popen call inside your renamed edit_files method, and keep process_priority in edit()'s signature/Editor(...) call alongside your filenames normalization. Done on my end.
